topic: The one money rule that made me rich
hook_summary: Stop saving money. It's keeping you broke.

scenes.audio_text: Stop saving money. You heard me. Saving is for people who want to stay average.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel in a bright coffee shop, natural window light, medium close-up, camera locked off, candid expression, slight smirk, holding a black coffee cup.

scenes.audio_text: When I was 22, I saved every penny. Had $20k in the bank. Felt great. Then I got laid off.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel walking on a city street at golden hour, over-shoulder shot, motion with walking, looking ahead, slight concern on face, denim jacket over polo.

scenes.audio_text: That $20k? Gone in 6 months. No skills, no network, no leverage. That's what saving gets you.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel sitting on a park bench, late afternoon sun, wide shot, hands gesturing, frustrated tone, casual clothes, trees in background.

scenes.audio_text: So I flipped it. Started spending every extra dollar on skills and relationships. Books, courses, coffee with smart people.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel in a modern library, medium close-up, slow push-in, confident expression, holding a book, warm ambient lighting.

scenes.audio_text: Now I make in a month what I used to save in a year. Spend to earn. That's the whole thing.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel in a sleek office with city view, locked-off medium shot, leaning forward, direct eye contact, dark polo, professional lighting.
